provenance.pin_detail: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

privilege_notes: runs fetched package code at launch

findings.title: Installed on-the-fly via a package runner
findings.detail: Server 'mcp-server-browserbase' is fetched and executed at launch via 'npx' with auto-accept flag(s) -y; package spec: @browserbasehq/mcp-server-browserbase.
findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

findings.title: Package source is unpinned or mutable
findings.detail: Server 'mcp-server-browserbase' package spec '@browserbasehq/mcp-server-browserbase' is unpinned (pin strength: none): no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now).
findings.recommendation: Pin an exact version (pkg@1.2.3) or a git commit SHA. Avoid @latest and branch refs.

evidence.reason: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

findings.title: Server requires sensitive credentials
findings.detail: Server 'mcp-server-browserbase' requires credential-like env keys: BROWSERBASE_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY.
findings.recommendation: Confirm each credential is scoped to least privilege and rotatable. Note which tools can exfiltrate it.

tools.name: browserbase_session_create
tools.description: Create or reuse a Browserbase browser session and set it as active.

tools.name: browserbase_session_close
tools.description: Close the current Browserbase session and reset the active context.

tools.name: browserbase_stagehand_navigate
tools.description:
Navigate to a URL in the browser. Only use this tool with URLs you're confident will work and be up to date. 
    Otherwise, use https://google.com as the starting point

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress
candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.

tools.name: browserbase_stagehand_act
tools.description: Perform a single action on the page (e.g., click, type).

candidate_capabilities.title: Reads secrets / credentials / env
candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that surfaces secrets pairs dangerously with any network_egress tool on the same server — read-then-send is a complete exfil chain.

data_categories.label: Secrets, tokens & credentials
data_categories.rationale: The most sensitive payload — leaks compromise everything downstream.

tools.name: browserbase_stagehand_extract
tools.description: Extract structured data or text from the current page using an instruction.

tools.name: browserbase_stagehand_observe
tools.description: Find interactive elements on the page from an instruction; optionally return an action.

tools.name: browserbase_screenshot
tools.description: Capture a full-page screenshot and return it (and save as a resource).

tools.name: browserbase_stagehand_get_url
tools.description: Return the current page URL (full URL with query/fragment).

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress
candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.
candidate_capabilities.validation_reason: The matched 'url' refers to retrieving the current page URL from browser state, not initiating network egress; no outbound network calls occur.

tools.name: browserbase_stagehand_agent
tools.description: Execute a task autonomously using Gemini Computer Use agent. The agent will navigate and interact with web pages to complete the given task.

candidate_capabilities.title: Arbitrary code / command execution

evidence.snippet: Execute a task autonomously using Gem

candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that runs arbitrary commands or code is the highest-impact surface an MCP server can expose — it collapses every other boundary.
candidate_capabilities.validation_reason: The matched 'Execute' means executing a task/goal through browser automation, not executing arbitrary code or shell commands.

data_categories.label: Project / task / status metadata

evidence.snippet: Execute a task autonomously using Gemini Com

data_categories.rationale: Names, statuses, and structure of work — identifying but not the underlying content.
data_categories.validation_reason: The matched 'task' is just the generic action the tool performs; it does not indicate the tool processes project metadata, issues, or tickets as data.

toxic_combinations.title: Read-then-send exfiltration chain
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can both access secrets/credentials and make outbound network calls — a path to exfiltrate them to an attacker-chosen host.

toxic_combinations.title: Code execution alongside secret access
toxic_combinations.detail: Arbitrary execution combined with credential access means a single tool call can read and abuse every secret the server holds.
toxic_combinations.validation_reason: The code_execution component is false_positive (tool does task automation, not code execution), so the toxic combination does not hold.

validated_out.reason: The matched 'url' refers to retrieving the current page URL from browser state, not initiating network egress; no outbound network calls occur.
The matched 'Execute' means executing a task/goal through browser automation, not executing arbitrary code or shell commands.
The matched 'task' is just the generic action the tool performs; it does not indicate the tool processes project metadata, issues, or tickets as data.
The code_execution component is false_positive (tool does task automation, not code execution), so the toxic combination does not hold.
